name: Heeia
desc: Coastal community in Kaneohe Bay with a working ancient fishpond — rich cultural heritage, lush, and deeply local.
insider: Heeia Fishpond is a working 800-year-old Hawaiian fishpond you can visit and volunteer at — a remarkable living cultural experience
Heeia State Park has a free kayak and canoe launch into Kaneohe Bay — bring your own or rent from nearby outfitters
The community is deeply connected to Hawaiian cultural practices, land stewardship, and native plant restoration
Views across Kaneohe Bay to Chinaman's Hat (Mokolii Island) are stunning from the shoreline park and pier
Heeia Kea Pier was once a working boat harbor — the old pier area is a peaceful spot to fish or just sit and watch the bay
Paepae o Heeia (the fishpond restoration project) welcomes volunteers — it's the best way to connect with the community
The wetlands behind the fishpond are being restored with native Hawaiian plants — birdwatching is excellent here
Jungle Juice Bar near Heeia Kea Pier has fresh smoothies and acai bowls — a hidden gem that most people drive right past
He'eia was part of an ancient ahupua'a stretching from the mountains to the sea — the fishponds, lo'i kalo, and reef systems here sustained large Hawaiian populations for centuries
Kaneohe Bay from here has the only barrier reef in Hawaii — the snorkeling offshore is incredible if you have a boat or kayak
